ogTitle: Main Beach vs Majors Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Main Beach and Majors Creek.
heading: Main Beach vs Majors Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Main Beach, Queensland and Majors Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Majors Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Main Beach (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Main Beach or Majors Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Main Beach has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Majors Creek scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Main Beach scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Majors Creek scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Main Beach or Majors Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Majors Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
